title: Baton LLM Check Report
description: The structured report returned by any of Baton's six deterministic LLM check types (spec-ambiguity, design-review, ac-satisfaction, security-review, semantic-coverage, maintainability-review). Emitted as a schema-constrained structured-output call (ADR-0011), never scraped from prose. Grading uses two ORTHOGONAL fields: `severity` is impact (one scale across all six checks) and `blocking` is disposition (does this finding fail the check?). Conflating them is what allowed a critical finding to ride alongside a self-declared PASS. The verdict is therefore DERIVED, not independently asserted: it is FAIL if and only if at least one finding is blocking. Both directions are enforced below, so a PASS carrying a blocking finding is schema-invalid.

verdict.description: DERIVED from the findings, never independently asserted. FAIL if and only if at least one finding has blocking: true. An engine that disagrees with the model's stated verdict must fail closed.

severity.description: IMPACT — how bad is this, if real? One scale across all six checks. Says nothing about whether the check fails; that is `blocking`.

blocking.description: DISPOSITION — does this finding fail the check? Each check's prompt defines which findings block. A blocking finding forces verdict: FAIL.

allOf.title: A FAIL verdict must be backed by at least one blocking finding.
A PASS verdict may not carry a blocking finding. This is the fail-open that the two-vocabulary grading allowed: a critical finding riding alongside a self-declared PASS.
